content.full_text:
)

U-4305
m-79-203

When does a copy of a mass mailing have to be

sent to the Commission?

Whenever it expressly advocates a result in an

election with respect to a clearly identified candidate or

measure.

See Opinion requested by Juvinall, 2 FPPC Ops.

110. Unlike the first sentence, the second sentence is

applicable regardless of whether the person has reporting

obligations. This policy was agreed upon at the opinion

request meeting of May 14, 1976, and set forth by Natalie

to a fellow in Minnesota.

)

)

sections.conclusion: Whenever it expressly advocates a result in an election with respect to a clearly identified candidate or measure.
sections.question_synthetic: When must a copy of a mass mailing be submitted to the Fair Political Practices Commission?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Mass mailing copies must be sent to the Commission when they expressly advocate for or against a clearly identified candidate or measure in an election, regardless of whether the sender has other reporting obligations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers. Text appears fragmentary with apparent OCR or transcription errors ('ide fied' for 'identified', unclear date reference '976'). Policy statement attributed to meeting of May 14, 1976. Reference citation to Juvinall opinion provided. Some content appears incomplete or corrupted.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: When must a copy of a mass mailing be submitted to the Fair Political Practices Commission?

CONCLUSION: Whenever it expressly advocates a result in an election with respect to a clearly identified candidate or measure.
embedding.first_500_words: ) U-4305 m-79-203 When does a copy of a mass mailing have to be sent to the Commission? Whenever it expressly advocates a result in an election with respect to a clearly identified candidate or measure. See Opinion requested by Juvinall, 2 FPPC Ops. 110. Unlike the first sentence, the second sentence is applicable regardless of whether the person has reporting obligations. This policy was agreed upon at the opinion request meeting of May 14, 1976, and set forth by Natalie to a fellow in Minnesota. ) )
embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter addressing the requirement for submitting mass mailing copies to the Commission, specifying that submission is required when mailings expressly advocate regarding identified candidates or measures.
